seoTitle: Tea Gardens vs Telegraph Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Tea Gardens vs Telegraph Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tea Gardens and Telegraph Point.
heading: Tea Gardens vs Telegraph Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tea Gardens, New South Wales and Telegraph Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Telegraph Point has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Tea Gardens (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tea Gardens or Telegraph Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tea Gardens has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Telegraph Point scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tea Gardens scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Telegraph Point scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tea Gardens or Telegraph Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Telegraph Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
